title: CResetPwdHandler and Security Question Pre-Auth Analysis

pre_auth_confirmation.method: Scanned 0x4c7400-0x4cad00 for BL instructions targeting CheckWebSession (0x4c1bd8)
pre_auth_confirmation.result: ZERO calls to CheckWebSession in entire CResetPwdHandler range
pre_auth_confirmation.verdict: CResetPwdHandler is PRE-AUTH by design (handles password reset for locked-out users)

memory_safety_audit.verdict: All string operations are bounded. strncpy with explicit limits, snprintf with size parameter. Buffers pre-zeroed with memset. No exploitable stack overflow in handler code.

findings.title: Pre-Auth Security Question Brute Force (No Rate Limiting)
findings.description: The checkQuestionAnswer handler performs security question verification via a backend IPasswdFind interface (vtable[0x58/4]) without any rate limiting, lockout mechanism, or CAPTCHA. An attacker can send unlimited requests to brute-force security question answers for any user account.
findings.timing_safety: Comparison delegated to backend -- likely NOT timing-safe (standard strcmp pattern)
findings.novelty_check: NOVEL -- [REDACTED-ID]_F014 was checkAuthCode prediction, NOT security question brute force
findings.impact: Enumerate all security question answers for any account including admin

reproduction.step_1: Call DevInit.getStatus to check if SecretQuestion is configured
reproduction.step_2: Call PasswdFind.getSecretQuestion to retrieve configured questions
reproduction.step_3: Call PasswdFind.checkQuestionAnswer repeatedly with different answers
reproduction.step_4: No lockout -- continue until correct answer found

findings.title: Pre-Auth Password Reset via Security Questions
findings.description: Once security question answers are known (via [REDACTED-ID]_F001), an attacker can reset any user's password without knowing the current password. The handler accepts user, question/answer array, new password, and password tip.
findings.novelty_check: NOVEL -- Rhea-exclusive method not present in Themis V2.620
findings.impact: Complete account takeover of any user including admin
findings.attack_chain: [REDACTED-ID]_F001 (brute force answers) -> [REDACTED-ID]_F002 (reset password) -> full device access

findings.title: Pre-Auth Device Info Disclosure via getBasicInfo
findings.description: Returns device serial number and vendor without authentication.
findings.novelty_check: Similar to [REDACTED-ID]_F003 (magicBox.getSystemInfo) but different endpoint and data. Marginal novelty.
findings.impact: Leaks serial number for targeted attacks and checkAuthCode derivation

findings.title: Pre-Auth Device Status Disclosure via getStatus
findings.impact: Reveals device initialization state and attack surface (whether security questions are set, what recovery methods exist)
findings.enables: [REDACTED-ID]_F001 attack -- attacker knows whether to target security questions or auth codes

findings.title: Pre-Auth Encryption Info and Public Key Disclosure
findings.impact: Reveals encryption scheme for password reset flows, enabling proper payload construction

findings.title: Pre-Auth Device Initialization Race Condition
findings.description: On uninitialized devices, this handler allows setting the admin password and configuring security questions without any authentication. Backend may enforce one-time initialization constraint, but handler itself does not check.
findings.condition: Device not yet initialized (factory fresh or after reset)
findings.impact: First network attacker to reach device after reset can claim admin credentials

console_runCmd.verdict: AUTHENTICATED -- session check present in all console handlers

autoSearchIP.bounds_checks: cmp r7, #127 before memcpy; cmp.w r9, #512 before copy; cmp.w r0, #65536
autoSearchIP.verdict: BOUNDED -- explicit size checks before all copy operations

mDNS_bonjour.verdict: LOW RISK -- Apple library, Dahua wrapper is service registration only

rtsp_over_http_auth.default: Could not determine statically -- set via runtime config
